topics.title: Deep learning foundations
topics.rationale: The first AI research thread is the question behind neural networks themselves: what kinds of functions can trainable systems represent, and what architectural biases make that representation practical?

sources.label: George Cybenko, Approximation by Superpositions of a Sigmoidal Function
Essay: The mental models of deep learning

topics.title: Architectures for representation learning
topics.rationale: CNNs and transformers show the central AI research move: choose a computation pattern that matches the structure of the data, then train the whole pipeline end to end.

sources.label: Yann LeCun et al., Gradient-Based Learning Applied to Document Recognition
Ashish Vaswani et al., Attention Is All You Need

topics.title: Efficient model adaptation
topics.rationale: Large models make adaptation a first-class research problem: how much can a model specialize without retraining every parameter or adding unacceptable inference cost?

sources.label: Hu et al., LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

topics.title: Controllable generation and diffusion language models
topics.rationale: Controllable generation asks whether generation can be steered by explicit conditions while preserving fluency, diversity, and task usefulness.

sources.label: Li et al., Diffusion-LM Improves Controllable Text Generation

topics.title: Memory and test-time learning
topics.rationale: Frontier AI systems increasingly need mechanisms for long context, persistent memory, and adaptation during use rather than only during pretraining.

sources.label: Google Research, Titans and MIRAS: helping AI have long-term memory

topics.title: Deep reinforcement learning
topics.rationale: Deep RL studies how trainable systems can learn useful policies from delayed, sparse, and changing feedback instead of fixed labeled datasets.

sources.label: Mnih et al., Playing Atari with Deep Reinforcement Learning

topics.title: Reinforcement learning for reasoning
topics.rationale: Reasoning-focused RL turns model behavior into an optimization target, raising questions about rewards, emergence, distillation, and where process supervision matters.

sources.label: DeepSeek-AI, DeepSeek-R1
DeepSeek-AI, DeepSeekMath

topics.title: Explainable AI as model evaluation
topics.rationale: Interpretability matters most when accuracy is not enough: deployment, trust, scientific discovery, fairness, and debugging all require sharper explanations.

sources.label: Doshi-Velez and Kim, Towards A Rigorous Science of Interpretable Machine Learning
Essay: Peeking Inside the Black Box

topics.title: Feature attribution and explanation skepticism
topics.rationale: LIME, SHAP, saliency maps, and their critiques form a useful testbed for asking whether explanations are faithful, stable, and actually useful to users.

sources.label: Ribeiro et al., Why Should I Trust You?
Adebayo et al., Sanity Checks for Saliency Maps

topics.title: Representation analysis and probing
topics.rationale: Representation analysis asks what information is encoded inside a model, how it changes across layers, and whether probes reveal structure or only probe capacity.

sources.label: Bengio et al., Representation Learning: A Review and New Perspectives
Hewitt and Liang, Designing and Interpreting Probes with Control Tasks

topics.title: Mechanistic interpretability
topics.rationale: Mechanistic interpretability is the attempt to reverse-engineer neural networks as computation: circuits, features, induction heads, superposition, and scalable tooling.

sources.label: Elhage et al., A Mathematical Framework for Transformer Circuits
Elhage et al., Toy Models of Superposition

topics.title: Agent evaluation, tool use, and verification
topics.rationale: Agent research should be measured by accuracy, cost, latency, repeatability, tool behavior, and whether verification loops catch errors before they compound.

sources.label: Kapoor et al., AI Agents That Matter
Yao et al., ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models

topics.title: Routing, model fusion, and efficient specialization
topics.rationale: Mixture-of-experts, cascades, model merging, task arithmetic, and speculative decoding all ask how to activate only the computation a task deserves.

sources.label: Shazeer et al., Outrageously Large Neural Networks
Leviathan et al., Fast Inference from Transformers via Speculative Decoding
